data.title: SA analyst: AI stocks lift market despite oil and yield pressures
data.description:
Wall Street’s muted start to the week masked a market being pulled in competing directions, as investors positioned themselves for a heavy slate of catalysts.

“Investors continue to chase the AI capex beneficiaries… This is keeping the overall sentiment positive, despite the rising oil prices and rising 10Y yields,” noted Seeking Alpha analyst Damir Tokic, underscoring the tension between optimism and emerging risks.

That dynamic was reflected in Monday’s price action as the S&P 500 (SP500) edged up +0.1%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) also gained +0.1% on the trading day and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) ended lower by -0.1%.

Tokic pointed to companies tied to AI infrastructure—particularly memory, storage, and chipmakers—as key drivers of sentiment, with standout momentum in firms like Micron (MU), SanDisk (SNDK), and Nvidia (NVDA) pushing markets toward record levels. 

However, Tokic warned that rising oil prices and climbing Treasury yields are beginning to signal inflationary pressures tied to the evolving U.S.-Iran conflict.

Furthermore, the analyst went on to say: “The VIX index remains just below 20 in advance of the Fed meeting and key tech earnings, both of which could boost volatility this week.” 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Sanmina Non-GAAP EPS of $3.16 beats by $0.76, revenue of $4.01B beats by $740M
data.description:
• Sanmina press release (SANM): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.16 beats by $0.76.
  • Revenue of $4.01B (+102.5% Y/Y) beats by $740M.

  • Third Quarter Fiscal 2026 Outlook

    • Revenue between $3.2 billion to $3.5 billion vs $3.51B consensus
    • Non-GAAP operating margin between 6.4% to 6.9%
    • Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share between $2.55 to $2.85 vs $2.53 consensus

  • Fiscal Year 2026 Outlook

    • Revenue between $13.7 billion to $14.3 billion vs $13.72B consensus
    • Non-GAAP operating margin between 6.3% to 6.6%
    • Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share between $10.75 to $11.35 vs $10.02 consensus
  • Shares +3.3%.

data.title: Bed Bath & Beyond GAAP EPS of -$0.24 beats by $0.04, revenue of $248M beats by $3.04M
data.description:
• Bed Bath & Beyond press release (BBBY): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.24 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $248M (+7.0% Y/Y) beats by $3.04M.
  • Gross profit was $59 million, or 23.9% of net revenue. Sales & Marketing expense was $32 million, or 13.0% of net revenue, a 50 basis point reduction year-overyear.
  • Technology and general and administrative expense declined to $36 million compared to $41 million in the prior year, a $5 million improvement year-over-year.
  • Net loss was $16 million, a $24 million improvement year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA (non-GAAP) was ($8) million, a $5 million improvement year-over-year.
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash totaled $163 million at quarter end.

data.title: Domino's CEO sees rivals reporting weaker Q1 sales
data.description:
Domino's Pizza (DPZ) CEO Russell Weiner said he expects rival fast-food chains to report lower-than-expected quarterly sales due in part to winter weather issues and weakened customer sentiment.

“One of the bad things about reporting first is you don’t get to hear about anybody else,” Weiner told CNBC in an interview on Monday.

Earlier Monday, Domino's reported weaker-than-expected Q1 same-store sales and lowered its full-year U.S. same-store sales forecast. The pizza maker was the first major fast-food company to roll out its Q1 earnings report, CNBC noted.

"We’re not happy with it,” Weiner told CNBC, referring to his company's quarterly report. 

Other fast-food companies reporting earnings this week include Starbucks (SBUX), Pizza Hut owner Yum! (YUM), and Chipotle (CMG), with Papa John's (PZZA) reporting next week. 

Weiner said he expects rivals Pizza Hut and Papa John's to report lower Q1 same-store sales despite running promotions during the quarter. 

“Domino’s has got a bigger advertising budget than our second two competitors combined,” he told CNBC. “And those competitors are both going up for sale, so we know things aren’t good there right now.”

data.title: Federal Reserve ‘doesn’t need to do much’ - Piper Sandler
data.description:
The Federal Reserve has little reason to adjust interest rates in the near term because the U.S. economy remains on solid footing despite recent uncertainty, according to Nancy Lazar, chief global economist at Piper Sandler.

“At the end of the day, the economy is fine,” Lazar said in a CNBC interview Monday. “The Fed has already cut rates. There’s fiscal support. So at the end of the day, we don’t think the Fed really needs to do much of anything.”

Lazar pointed to improving economic indicators as evidence the economy is weathering recent turbulence. After a soft first quarter with gross domestic product growth around 2%, she said signs of acceleration are emerging in the second quarter.

“The manufacturing indices we’ve seen from the regional Fed have been all, pretty much on the stronger side,” Lazar said. “Our daily consumer confidence survey after going down some in the month of March has actually stabilized, improved here in April.”

Meanwhile, Lazar emphasized that the Fed has already provided substantial monetary stimulus, cutting rates by 175 basis points last year. She noted that money supply is accelerating, bank loans are growing and lending standards have eased incrementally.

Markets are pricing in no rate cut through at least late July, with a near-60% probability the Fed holds steady. 

“Bond yields (US2Y) (US10Y) have been basically unchanged for almost three years, either not suggesting the Fed is being too tight or too easy,” Lazar said. “So steady as she goes, I think, is a pretty good outlook for the next at least nine to twelve months.”

Piper Sandler is projecting GDP growth of 3% for 2026, above consensus estimates, supported by fiscal measures including capital expenditure incentives, full depreciation allowances and tax refunds.

data.title: MSC Industrial could hit highest-ever close as KeyBanc upgrades to Overweight
data.description:
MSC Industrial (MSM) up 6.3% in Monday's trading, poised to possibly reach its highest-ever closing price, as KeyBanc upgraded the industrial equipment distributor to Overweight from Sector Weight with a $117 price target, following investor meetings with new CEO Martina McIsaac.

KeyBanc analyst Ken Newman said MSC Industrial (MSM) has a clear line of sight toward structurally improving its cost structure in the coming years, saying the company should generate stronger operating leverage through improving cycle volumes and additional cost reductions, adding that company management indicated plans for an incremental reduction of ~1,000 positions in back-office and warehouse-related roles, which could total ~$50M in run-rate savings through FY 2028.

Assuming a stronger cycle inflection, Newman said he thinks earnings power could exceed $6/share by FY 2027 in a bull-case scenario.

The analyst said he has long viewed MSC Industrial (MSM) as having one of the best "coiled springs" within the bank's distributor coverage in the event of a cycle inflection, with much of the leverage opportunity stemming from multiple years of structural changes made during the company's Mission Critical 2.0 initiatives to re-engage the core customer set, which accounts for a little more than half of total sales.

data.title: Eli Lilly Q1 Preview: Obesity portfolio keeps analysts bullish; Foundayo in focus
data.description:
Eli Lilly (LLY) is expected to report a sharp year-over-year increase in first-quarter earnings, with analysts viewing the drugmaker as well-positioned in the obesity treatment market.

Lilly will report its results on April 30, during market hours. The consensus EPS Estimate is $6.85, while revenue is expected to jump 40.3% Y/Y to $17.86B.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 2 upward revisions and 9 downward moves, while revenue estimates have seen 8 upward revisions and 1 downward move.

Seeking Alpha analyst Oliver Rodzianko says LLY's medium-term growth is anchored by obesity drugs Mounjaro and Zepbound, with Foundayo and the $7B acquisition of Kelonia Therapeutics as near-term catalysts.

The oral weight loss drug Foundayo was approved on April 1 and started shipping via LillyDirect on April 6. Although it may not impact Q1, analysts believe management’s commentary will be essential to evaluate it against competitor Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy pill.

Another analyst, Stephen Ayers, said he will be keenly looking forward to the management’s commentary, especially if Lilly reaffirms its 2026 revenue guidance, which he believes will provide an insight into Foundayo’s expected performance.

“If they stay there, it may signal to the market that Foundayo is tracking, but if it's narrowed to the low end, it could be a sign that either pricing erosion or Foundayo uptake is lagging,” he said.

Additional updates on Eli Lilly’s next-generation injectable obesity treatment, retatrutide, will also be an area of focus.

Over the last 2 years, BMY has beaten EPS and revenue estimates 100% of the time, setting up expectations of another bumper quarter. The stock has declined more than 17% in the year so far.

data.title: Unity's Vector continues to expand as it prepares to sunset ironSource: BTIG
data.description:
Unity Software (U) appears poised to continue making gains with Vector, its AI-powered ad platform launched last year, as the company prepares to sunset its ironSource advertising network, according to BTIG.

Shares were up 2.5% during afternoon trading on Monday.

The software company plans to retire the ironSource Ads Network on April 30 and has engaged with financial advisors to assist with the divestiture of its Supersonic game publishing business.

BTIG views the moves as favorable, reiterating its Buy rating and $39 price target on the stock.

"Based on our analysis of the ironSource purchase consideration and '21-22 changes in amortization schedule, we believe there could be up to $221M in amortization savings post-close/sale with de minimis depreciation impact (just $7M of PP&E within the purchase price allocation)," said BTIG analysts Clark Lampen and Joseph Spiezio in a Monday investor note. "Applying those savings to our Vector base and bull case forecasts, we see ~$0.20-0.60 in earnings potential for the Vector operation alone in '27 as non-cash drags moderate."

Unity plans to release its first quarter 2026 financial results on May 7. A consensus estimate expects adjusted earnings per share of $0.24 and revenue of $504.25M.

In March, Unity increased its first-quarter guidance. Unity expects revenue to be between $505M and $508M, compared to guidance of $480M to $490M. Create revenue is expected to be around $115M, while Grow revenue is expected to be around $353M. Analysts had anticipated sales of $488.92M.

data.title: MARA Foundation launch hits stock: Bitcoin's $100K vote war begins
data.description:
MARA Holdings (MARA) announced on Monday the launch of its new MARA Foundation at the Bitcoin 2026 conference in Las Vegas.

The goal is simple: support Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) long-term growth beyond mining—through research, education, and open-source development.

The foundation will focus on key areas like Bitcoin security (including quantum resistance research), open-source development, global self-custody tools, policy advocacy, and BTC education for developers and regulators.

In short, MARA Holdings (MARA) is trying to strengthen Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) “base layer” so the network becomes more secure and widely usable over time.

To kick things off, the company also announced a $100K community grant vote, where the Bitcoin (BTC-USD) community will choose one of three organizations:

  • 256 Foundation funds open-source Bitcoin mining hardware/software and developer education.
  • Libreria de Satoshi spreads BTC technical education globally in multiple languages.
  • SateNet builds community-run internet networks in the Global South using BTC support.

This matters because it shows mining giants like MARA (MARA) are moving beyond just earning Bitcoin (BTC-USD)—they are now actively funding the ecosystem that keeps the world's largest cryptocurrency running.

On the treasury side, MARA (MARA) currently holds about 38.689K BTC, making it the 4th largest Bitcoin treasury holder, according to Coingecko. It trails Strategy (MSTR) with ~818.334K BTC, Twenty One Capital (XXI) with ~43.514K, and Metaplanet (MPJPY) with ~40.177K. This puts the company firmly among the top institutional Bitcoin (BTC-USD) holders.

Despite the announcement, the stock fell nearly ~4% to $11.20, showing short-term market pressure.
